sections.title: A: Complete with modal perfect forms
sections.instructions: Use the correct modal perfect form of the verbs in brackets.

items.sentence: Olivia had plenty of free time, so she ...... (help) us with the decorations.
Jake looks terrible today. He ...... (rest) properly last night.
You ...... (warn) me about the traffic. We missed the concert!
The lights were already off, so they ...... (go) to bed early.
I ...... (reserve) a table earlier, but I completely forgot.
Nina ...... (stay) at home yesterday because I saw her downtown.
You ...... (not / say) those rude things to your sister.
Tom ...... (leave) his notebook in the classroom by accident.

sections.title: B: Choose the correct option
sections.instructions: Circle the correct modal phrase in each sentence.

items.sentence: We **could have / should have** taken a taxi, but we decided to walk.
She **might have / must have** been nervous before the interview.
They **can't have / shouldn't have** finished already — it was a huge project.
You **may have / should have** asked permission before borrowing my laptop.
Lucas **might have / ought to have** forgotten to lock the door.
I **must have / could have** passed the test if I had studied harder.
Mum **must have / should have** taken the wrong train. She's very late.
You **can't have / ought to have** been kinder to your cousin.

sections.title: C: Criticism and expectation
sections.instructions: Complete with should have, shouldn't have or ought to have.

items.sentence: You ...... worn a warmer coat. It's freezing outside.
We ...... eaten so much before the trip.
I ...... paid more attention during the lesson.
They ...... laughed at him in front of everyone.
The train ...... arrived by now.
You ...... explained the situation more clearly.
He ...... lied to his teacher.
My package ...... come yesterday.

sections.title: D: Probability and possibility
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with must have, can't have, may have or might have.

items.sentence: All the windows are open. Someone ...... forgotten to close them.
Emma isn't in her room. She ...... gone out with her friends.
Ryan answered every question perfectly. He ...... studied really hard.
That smell ...... come from the fridge — it's completely empty.
They ...... taken the earlier bus, so they arrived before us.
This email ...... arrived this morning because I checked my inbox earlier.
Laura looks relieved. She ...... solved the problem.
My wallet isn't here. I ...... dropped it at the café.

sections.title: E: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake with a modal perfect. Find the bold word and write the correct form.

items.sentence: You should **spoke** to the manager earlier.
They might **had** missed the bus.
I could have **ate** dinner before we left.
She can't have **wrote** that message.
We ought **have to** checked the map first.
He shouldn't have **drived** so fast.
It may have **went** wrong somehow.
You must have **see** that film already.

sections.title: F: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning.

items.sentence:
I'm sure Ethan lost his keys. **MUST**
Ethan ...... his keys.

It wasn't a good idea to ignore her messages. **SHOULDN'T**
You ...... her messages.

Perhaps Chloe forgot her homework at home. **MIGHT**
Chloe ...... her homework at home.

I had the opportunity to join the team, but I didn't. **COULD**
I ...... the team, but I didn't.

It is impossible that Noah broke the window. **CAN'T**
Noah ...... the window.

It was wrong not to thank her. **OUGHT**
You ...... her.

sections.title: G: Error correction
sections.instructions: Find and correct one mistake in each sentence.

items.sentence: She might have **lose** her phone.
He can't have **went** home so early.
You should have **gave** me a call.
They may have **forget** the address.
We could have **done** better if we had more time.
I ought to have **saw** the warning sign.

sections.title: H: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using a maximum of four words, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence.

items.sentence:
I'm sure he didn't steal the bicycle.
He ...... the bicycle.

It was wrong of you to interrupt the teacher.
You ...... the teacher.

I'm sure they were tired after the journey.
They ...... tired after the journey.

Perhaps she missed the beginning of the film.
She ...... the beginning of the film.

It was a pity you didn't apply for the job.
You ...... for the job.

I'm sure the exam wasn't easy.
It ...... easy.
